title: Dangers of missing WHERE
summary: Dangers of missing WHERE in Updating and Deleting Data

sketchBlocks.title: Why WHERE is Important in DELETE and UPDATE
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When you run an UPDATE or DELETE statement in SQL, the WHERE clause determines which rows are affected. If you forget to include a WHERE clause, **every row in the table** will be updated or deleted. This can lead to accidental data loss or unwanted changes. For example:

```sql
-- Dangerous! Deletes all items
DELETE FROM inventory_items;

-- Safe: Deletes only test items
DELETE FROM inventory_items WHERE is_test = 1;
```

Always double-check your WHERE clause before running data-changing commands.

sketchBlocks.title: Preview Before You Change
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
A safe habit is to run a SELECT query with your intended WHERE clause before running UPDATE or DELETE. This lets you preview which rows will be affected:

```sql
SELECT name, price, id, price * id AS calculated_value
FROM inventory_items;
```

This helps prevent accidental changes to the wrong data.

quizDraft.title: Consequences of Missing WHERE
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following can happen if you run a DELETE statement without a WHERE clause? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what happens when no filter is applied.

help.concept: A DELETE without WHERE affects every row, causing total data loss. Recovery may require backups.
help.hint_1: No WHERE means no filter—every row is deleted.
help.hint_2: Losing all rows can mean lost data and the need for backup restoration.

quizDraft.title: Purpose of WHERE in DELETE
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank: The WHERE clause in a DELETE statement is used to ______.
quizDraft.template: The WHERE clause in a DELETE statement is used to {blank}.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how WHERE limits which data is affected.

help.concept: WHERE specifies which rows are targeted in UPDATE or DELETE statements.
help.hint_1: It acts as a filter for the operation.
help.hint_2: Without WHERE, all rows are affected; with WHERE, only matching rows are changed.

quizDraft.title: Delete All Rows (Dangerous Example)
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL statement that deletes all rows from the inventory_items table. (This is dangerous and should be avoided in real scenarios!)
quizDraft.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL statement to delete only the rows in inventory_items where is_test is 1.
